observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit all her collections intact on a daily basis or not later than the next banking day and desist from the practice of accumulating huge amount of cash balances in her custody pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  4, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the BAC and the Municipal Accountant (MA) to ensure that supporting documents are complete and faithfully accomplished in accordance with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.
The concerned officials agreed and committed to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Health Officer continue the efficient discharge and safeguard of COVID-19 vaccines so that total immunization in the locality can be obtained in the future.
The concerned officials agreed and committed to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team (AT).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) install internal control over the handling of collections and deposits by the Municipal Treasurer in order to safeguard assets and promote efficient operations in consonance with the fiscal responsibility of the Agency Head provided by Section 124 of PD No. 1445; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor at the end of each month the mandatory remittance of collections by the Municipal Treasurer in consonance with the Accountant’s responsibility to install and maintain an internal audit system in the local government unit pursuant to Section 474(b) of RA No. 7160; (c) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) adjust the cashbook balance for the reconciling items noted in the Reconciliation Statement of Accountability; and to (ii) check the correctness of individual Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) against corresponding official receipts before accepting collections from the liquidating officer and the individual collectors; and (d) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) record the transactions for the Cash Local Treasury account in a timely manner; and (ii) perform reconciliation of accounting and treasury records on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  3, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the MA to present the Cash in Bank – LCTD in the Investment account; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) verify and communicate with the bank as to the sources of the Unrecorded/unaccounted deposits totaling to ₱421,016.51 for proper recognition in the books; (ii) cause the immediate submission of all paid DVs and supporting documents totaling ₱17,993,787.73 to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; (iii) exercise due diligence in the performance of her official duties and responsibilities as a Municipal Treasurer  by desisting from the practice of entrusting paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to unauthorized persons; (c) require the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded/unaccounted deposits totaling to ₱421,016.51  into a temporary trust liability account which shall be adjusted  in the books  based on the result of the Municipal Treasurer’s verification with the bank;  (d) re-activate or close the dormant PVB account to avoid incurrence of dormancy charges.  

In addition, we recommended the LCE to provide timeline for the submission of the said DVs, otherwise, require the suspension of payment of the salaries of the MT and other concerned officials.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  (a)  the Municipal Accountant to reclassify  the Small Value Items in the PPE accounts as Inventories upon purchase and recognize them as an expense upon issuance to the end-user; (b) the Municipal Engineer  to assess the present value of the damaged constructed overflow bridge at Sitio Abo, Pulong Sampaloc and to report to the Municipal Accountant  its   impaired   condition for proper disclosure and/or  derecognition in the books pursuant to the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 21 or the Impairment of Non-Cash Generating Assets; and (c) the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the procurement of solar lights amounting to ₱5,241,502.00 under the PPE, Power Supply Systems account and to recognize  the asset  under  the Construction Materials Inventory account.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  7, Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) designate an officer/employee  to be fully in-charge in  the receipt, issuances, monitoring of inventory items, and the preparation and submission of the required reports pursuant to  existing regulations; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer, being  the property and supply officer,  to reconcile the balance of the inventory accounts per books and to submit the necessary RIS and RSMI to reflect the accurate balance of the account; (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the erroneous recording of the accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2020; (d) require the Municipal Engineer to immediately program and provide timelines for the installation of the solar lights for the benefit of the constituents; (e) limit the procurement of solar lights and other supplies and materials  to what is immediate and necessary to avoid wastage of government funds;  (f) construct an adequate storeroom/bodega to properly store  supplies and materials and protect the assets from the danger of pilferage, damage, and unnecessary loss; and (g)  limit access thereof to authorized personnel only.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the BAC to (i) strictly conduct eligibility screening and to directly negotiate or procure only from a legally, technically, and financially capable supplier before entering into a contract; and (ii) properly evaluate  programs, projects, and activities before deciding  the most suitable or  appropriate  methods of procurement that it deems fit based on its needs and requirements; (b) to allow/approve only disbursement claims which are supported by appropriately  filled-up documents necessary to prove the validity, propriety, and legality of the transaction; and (c) adhere strictly with  the spirit and intent of the provisions of  RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and related issuances with the objective of achieving transparency and promoting competition to obtain the most advantageous price and terms for the Municipality.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that  the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning Officer and Municipal Engineer to (a) properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for the timely implementation and completion of  planned programs, projects and activities; (b) ensure the regular monitoring of  the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and (c) the other implementing  officials concerned to strictly observe the general guidelines and the reporting and monitoring on the information and publicity on PPAs of projects undertaken by the municipality in accordance with  COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: Similar and other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No.  8, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the BAC to strictly conduct eligibility screening of its suppliers and to properly evaluate the appropriate method to be used in its procurement system before deciding on the procurement modality to be used; and (b) transact business directly with suppliers who are technically, legally and financially capable to deliver the goods at the most advantageous price and contract for the Government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit   to the COA Audit Team the status report on the action taken on the COA Audit findings and Recommendations within sixty days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report to enable the COA Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the Management on the audit recommendations pursuant to Section 99 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 11260 or the 2019 General Appropriation Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials involved in the processing of transactions and claims to be more cautious in the performance of their duties and responsibilities to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit in the future.
